mission.title: kube-rs: declarative openapi validation rules
mission.description: declarative openapi validation rules. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-rs project
steps.description:
Verify your kube-rs version and configuration:
```bash
cargo --version
rustc --version
grep kube Cargo.toml 2>/dev/null
```
This feature requires a Rust development environment with the kube-rs crate as a dependency.

steps.title: Review kube-rs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-rs configuration:
```bash
grep -A5 '\[dependencies\]' Cargo.toml
cargo tree | grep kube
```
It would be really cool if we had a way to create declarative openapi validation rules ala:
- https://book.kubebuilder.io/reference/generating-crd.html 
-

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
cargo build 2>&1 | tail -20
cargo test 2>&1 | tail -20
```
Confirm the feature described in "declarative openapi validation rules" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
For #159

This might not be the way to go, but at least it's a way to learn how to do proc-macros. It's daunting to try and fix stuff in the existing `k8s-openapi-derive` crate, but might still do that.

For now, this serves as failing tests. We've ripped out everything but arg parsing from the original crate.

prerequisites.description: A Rust development environment with cargo and the kube-rs crate as a project dependency.
